misc/gods/gods-of-concord-of-aedryn/aery.title: Aeryn-Vox
misc/gods/gods-of-concord-of-aedryn/aery.description: Domains: Knowledge, Secrets, Prophecy, Magic Symbol: An open eye surrounded by a radiant sunburst She is the god of knowledge believed to have been the one who sent the astral libraries crashing down onto the elven planet of Aellalone, allowing them to create spaceflight and reach her domain in the sun. Her symbols are found in the silent libraries that dot the cosmos—guarded Data-Temples which float in quiet orbits, waiting to be found. The Voquari Ascendancy is actively searching for her prophesied scion, believing her will shall manifest the strongest of the living gods.Note Divine Font: Heal Divine Sanctification: can choose holy or unsanctified Divine Skill: Arcana Favored Weapon: ? Domains: Knowledge, Secrecy, ?, Magic Cleric Spells: 1st: Akashic Download; 3rd: Hyper Cognition; 7th Contingency

misc/gods/gods-of-concord-of-aedryn/drav.title: Drav'kyn
misc/gods/gods-of-concord-of-aedryn/drav.description:
Domains: Communication, Language, Silence, Exile
Symbol: A broken mask with a beam of light emerging from the mouth
Synopsis: Drav’kyn is the god of speech, miscommunication, and the power of language. Once said to be whole, their face was broken and their tongue cracked, scattering different speech across species and stars. Now, their worship splits between two sects—those who seek to unify language (universal translators, diplomats), and those who revere silence as the true divine expression (monastic orders who speak only through coded light or gesture). Their followers are called to check into abandoned relay stations, where broken signals still echo believing it may be their voice still lingering.Note
Divine Font: ?
Divine Sanctification: ?
Divine Skill: ?
Favored Weapon: ?
Domains: ?
Cleric Spells: ?

misc/gods/gods-of-concord-of-aedryn/elia.title: Eliathe
misc/gods/gods-of-concord-of-aedryn/elia.description:
Domains: Dreams, Mind, Emotion, Illusion
Symbol: A nebula shaped like a sleeping face
Synopsis: Eliathe is the goddess of dreams, the subconscious, and simulated realities. It is believed that her dreams shape remembrance streams themselves, and that lucid dreamers walk paths she carves through space. According to her followers it is through her thread that the galaxies may be connected in a harmonious fashion forming a neural network structure across galaxies. Her worshippers hold dreamfasting rituals, where they enter group simulations to commune with each other and her.Note
Divine Font: ?
Divine Sanctification: ?
Divine Skill: ?
Favored Weapon: ?
Domains: Dreams, ?
Cleric Spells: ?

misc/gods/gods-of-concord-of-aedryn/ithe.title: Ithex
misc/gods/gods-of-concord-of-aedryn/ithe.description:
Domains: Earth, Memory, Binding, Ancients
Symbol: A cracked stone disc carved with a spiral maze
Synopsis: Ithex is the god of buried truths and forgotten power—an ageless force revered before language, was founded by any spacefaring group known today. Their worship lingers in deep ruins and untranslatable glyphs, called upon by those who seek what was never meant to be found. No digital record of Ithex remains intact—their symbol corrupts files leaving only the physical.Note
Divine Font: ?
Divine Sanctification: ?
Divine Skill: ?
Favored Weapon: ?
Domains: Earth, ?
Cleric Spells: ?

misc/gods/gods-of-concord-of-aedryn/kael.title: Kael’Zar
misc/gods/gods-of-concord-of-aedryn/kael.description:
Domains: Technology, Forge, Innovation, Craft
Symbol: A segmented gauntlet made of a circuit core
Synopsis: Kael’Zar is the god of invention and cosmic craft, worshipped by machinists, weaponwrights, and builders of starships and megastructures. Her flame is said to burn at the heart of neutron stars, and her most devoted followers rest in forge-temples orbiting black holes, where time bends to perfect the divine craft observing the flow of her progress in still time.Note
Divine Font: ?
Divine Sanctification: ?
Divine Skill: ?
Favored Weapon: ?
Domains: ?
Cleric Spells: ?

misc/gods/gods-of-concord-of-aedryn/kely.title: Kelyx
misc/gods/gods-of-concord-of-aedryn/kely.description:
Domains: Time, Fate, Duality, Entropy, Lost Realities
Symbol: Two eclipsing moons spiraling in opposite directions
Synopsis: Kelyx represents the paradox of time and twin natures. Depicted as both a brother-sister pair and a singular entity, Kelyx is invoked in rituals involving fate manipulation, time travel, or alternate realities. Worshippers believe the god exists in all possible futures simultaneously, and cults have formed around opposing “halves” of Kelyx, each claiming a different cosmic destiny. One teaching is that all realities happen, it’s just a matter of which reality Kelyx drags your conscious to.Note
Divine Font: ?
Divine Sanctification: ?
Divine Skill: ?
Favored Weapon: ?
Domains: ?
Cleric Spells: ?

misc/gods/gods-of-concord-of-aedryn/myrr.title: Myrrhex
misc/gods/gods-of-concord-of-aedryn/myrr.description:
Domains: Chaos, Creation, Nature, Space
Symbol: A spiral galaxy curled within a blooming flower
Synopsis: Myrrhex is the primordial architect of wild creation — the womb of stars and the seed of galaxies. She is not just a goddess of nature, but of unshaped matter, cosmic chaos, and violent, blooming genesis. Systems disappearing from the sky and reappearing color shifted and full of life when traveled too despite no sign of life before the disappearance. She is believed to be the goddess of the Vesk homeworld, there they pray not for gifts from her but that they may survive her creation.Note
Divine Font: ?
Divine Sanctification: ?
Divine Skill: ?
Favored Weapon: ?
Domains: ?
Cleric Spells: ?

misc/gods/gods-of-concord-of-aedryn/n’zh.title: N’Zhereth
misc/gods/gods-of-concord-of-aedryn/n’zh.description:
Domains: Death, Darkness, Endings, Void
Symbol: A black ring devouring a field of stars
Synopsis: N’Zhereth is the god of endings, loss, and the cold hunger of space. He is a necessary god though not loved by many; all temples contain a corner to him. Necromancers and Psychopomps alike invoke his name in the stillness between stars. It is whispered that N’Zhereth devours ascenders who falter, and that the silence of deep space is his voice. He has no demi planes—only echoes, tombs, and the void.Note
Divine Font: ?
Divine Sanctification: ?
Divine Skill: ?
Favored Weapon: ?
Domains: ?
Cleric Spells: ?

misc/gods/gods-of-concord-of-aedryn/quor.title: Quorrax
misc/gods/gods-of-concord-of-aedryn/quor.description: Domains: Desire, Ambition, Addiction, Secrets Symbol: A closed eye with a swirling galaxy inside Synopsis: Quorrax is the patron of unchecked desire—power, knowledge, sensation. They whisper temptations into the ears of travelers. Followers often implant subdermal “hunger glyphs” that pulse when near something they deeply crave. While some see them as a necessary god—driving exploration and progress—others view them as a corrupting force. Following the arrival of the Hekath, open worship of Quorrax has become extremely uncommon, many religious believe that the Hekath are a plague created by Quorrax as the ultimate evolution of consumption.Note Divine Font: ? Divine Sanctification: ? Divine Skill: ? Favored Weapon: ? Domains: ? Cleric Spells: ?

misc/gods/gods-of-concord-of-aedryn/sole.title: Solenne
misc/gods/gods-of-concord-of-aedryn/sole.description:
Domains: Light, Healing, Sun, Ascension
Symbol: A radiant sun encircled by three golden rings
Synopsis: Solenne is the solar goddess of light and transcendence, worshipped by healers, mystics, and Solarian monks. Her clergy believe the soul ascends through cycles of radiant suffering and cosmic enlightenment. Pilgrims seek her grace through sun-walks—ritual spaceflights close to solar stellar bodies. Though technology exists to cover scars many of her followers wear their scars with pride to show the blessings of life that she bestowed upon them in the time of need.Note
Divine Font: ?
Divine Sanctification: ?
Divine Skill: ?
Favored Weapon: ?
Domains: Healing, Sun, ?
Cleric Spells: ?

misc/gods/gods-of-concord-of-aedryn/tare.title: Tarellon
misc/gods/gods-of-concord-of-aedryn/tare.description:
Domains: Community, Cities, Architecture, Stability
Symbol: A hexagonal city seen from above, with a central glowing spire
Synopsis: Tarellon is the god of foundations—of cities raised from dust, alliances forged in stone, and the unseen bonds that hold societies together. They are worshipped by urban planners, governors, and homesteaders who see civilization not as conquest, but as cooperation. The winds that howl through forgotten cities are said to be Tarellon mourning what could have been.Note
Divine Font: ?
Divine Sanctification: ?
Divine Skill: ?
Favored Weapon: ?
Domains: ?
Cleric Spells: ?

misc/gods/gods-of-concord-of-aedryn/thar.title: Tharos
misc/gods/gods-of-concord-of-aedryn/thar.description: Domains: War, Law, Protection, Authority Symbol: A flaming sword over a constellation of a warrior (The constellation changes based on location) Synopsis: Tharos is the god of law and battle, revered by soldiers and planetary governors alike. His worship spread after the fall of the Vasen Empire, whose legions claimed to fight under his gaze. Though all that remains of that empire are the war machines, the calculating robots still chant his prayers. His temples are fortress-monasteries, where verdicts are as sacred as oaths. Tharos teaches that war must serve order, not chaos, and his clergy often act as enforcers in warzones and failed colonies. Some claim he once walked among the Vesk during their first interstellar conquests.Note Divine Font: ? Divine Sanctification: ? Divine Skill: ? Favored Weapon: ? Domains: ? Cleric Spells: ?

misc/gods/gods-of-concord-of-aedryn/vel’.title: Vel’Taro
misc/gods/gods-of-concord-of-aedryn/vel’.description:
Domains: Beauty, Passion, Alcohol, Celebration
Symbol: A jeweled goblet overflowing with starlight
Synopsis: Vel’Taro is the god of indulgence, artistry, and divine intoxication, worshipped in taverns, theaters, and celebration halls across the stars. His faithful believe joy is sacred, and that beauty, drink, and love are the highest forms of worship. Followers track where he is through spilled sacred alcohol in zero gravity, tracking its flow.Note
Divine Font: ?
Divine Sanctification: ?
Divine Skill: ?
Favored Weapon: ?
Domains: Passion, ?
Cleric Spells: ?

misc/gods/gods-of-concord-of-aedryn/veyr.title: Veyra
misc/gods/gods-of-concord-of-aedryn/veyr.description:
Domains: Nature, Mutation, Life, Evolution
Symbol: A spiraling vine with crystalline blossoms
Synopsis: Veyra is the goddess of growth, mutation, and adaptation. She is honored in bio-engineering labs and jungle moons alike. Her worshippers believe life must change or perish, and embrace augmentation, symbiosis, and controlled mutation. She has strange temples grown from seed-vaults and bloom into massive organic cathedrals. Many uplifted species and altered creatures owe their lineage to her faithful, who see genetic change not as corruption—but as sacred revelation.Note
Divine Font: ?
Divine Sanctification: ?
Divine Skill: ?
Favored Weapon: ?
Domains: Nature, ?
Cleric Spells: ?

misc/gods/gods-of-concord-of-aedryn/xala.title: Xalaith
misc/gods/gods-of-concord-of-aedryn/xala.description: Domains: Pain, Resilience, Sacrifice, Rebirth Symbol: A thorned spine wrapped around a glowing heart Synopsis: Xalaith teaches that pain is the price of becoming, and only through endurance can transcendence be found. She is revered in conflict zones, hospice ships, and planetary refuges. Her followers believe that scars are holy glyphs and that every wound is a gateway to metamorphosis. Her sect sometimes clashes with Solenne’s, believing suffering is not to be healed, but embraced.Note Divine Font: ? Divine Sanctification: ? Divine Skill: ? Favored Weapon: ? Domains: ? Cleric Spells: ?

misc/gods/gods-of-concord-of-aedryn/zarn.title: Zarn
misc/gods/gods-of-concord-of-aedryn/zarn.description:
Domains: Travel, Trade, Luck, Portals
Symbol: A three-fingered hand offering a stargate
Synopsis: Zarn is the god of gambles and deals made at the edge of reason. He is worshipped in remembrance observation stations and deep-space trade hubs, where his shrines serve as both altars and marketplaces. Legend says he once sold the first remembrance stream to a mortal, and he continues to barter secrets for devotion. His field clerics often double as traders, smugglers, or gambler.Note
Divine Font: ?
Divine Sanctification: ?
Divine Skill: ?
Favored Weapon: ?
Domains: Travel, Luck, ?
Cleric Spells: ?

misc/gods/gods-of-the-solara-imperium/th.title: The Ashen Veil
misc/gods/gods-of-the-solara-imperium/th.description:
Domains: Memory, Grief, Defiance, Identity
Symbol: A folded banner wrapped in black thread, faintly stained with soot
Synopsis: The Ashen Veil is the residue of remembrance, the weight of names that should have faded but did not. It is not typically worshipped, only kept. A forbidden concept, it clings to erased history and burns quietly in those who refuse to forget. The Imperium calls it a fracture in unity; its keepers call it truth. Followers of the Ashen Veil believe that without knowing the past one cannot truly know the present.Note
Divine Font: ?
Divine Sanctification: ?
Divine Skill: ?
Favored Weapon: ?
Domains: ?
Cleric Spells: ?
misc/gods/gods-of-the-solara-imperium/th.sourcePath: Misc/Gods/Gods of the Solara Imperium/The Ashen Veil.md

misc/gods/gods-of-the-solara-imperium/th.title: The Emperor Ascendant
misc/gods/gods-of-the-solara-imperium/th.description: Domains: Will, Order, Legacy, Unity Symbol: A crown floating above a shadowless flame Synopsis: Solaran Emperor is not immortal, nor remembered. Upon their death, each Emperor Spirit willingly enters the Silent Continuum, a metaphysical state where their name, face, and deeds begin to fade, not by forgetfulness, but by divine design. This unraveling is called the Unchaining, a sacred effect that severs the past from the present. Over time, even their statues blur, their voice erased from recordings, their commands reduced to principles without attribution. What remains is not legacy, but momentum, an empire freed from nostalgia, bound only to the will of the now. To resist this erasure is considered a spiritual fracture; to seek memory of a past Emperor is to fracture unity and is considered a great heresy in either case. In the Solaran view, the future must not be ruled by the dead.Note Divine Font: ? Divine Sanctification: ? Divine Skill: ? Favored Weapon: ? Domains: ? Cleric Spells: ?
misc/gods/gods-of-the-solara-imperium/th.sourcePath: Misc/Gods/Gods of the Solara Imperium/The Emperor Ascendant.md

misc/gods/gods-of-the-solara-imperium/th.title: The Hollow Chorus
misc/gods/gods-of-the-solara-imperium/th.description:
Domains: Obedience, Silence, Devotion, Standardization
Symbol: An array of open mouths with no tongues
Synopsis: The Chorus speaks in unison, or not at all. It is the echo of doctrine passed perfectly through generations. Individual voice is distortion. Harmony is transmission. Where speech is synchronized, where movement is mirrored, the Chorus is present. It rewards those who vanish within it.Note
Divine Font: ?
Divine Sanctification: ?
Divine Skill: ?
Favored Weapon: ?
Domains: ?
Cleric Spells: ?
misc/gods/gods-of-the-solara-imperium/th.sourcePath: Misc/Gods/Gods of the Solara Imperium/The Hollow Chorus.md

misc/gods/gods-of-the-solara-imperium/th.title: The Living Throne
misc/gods/gods-of-the-solara-imperium/th.description:
Domains: Variable (Defined by the Reigning Solaran Emperor) Symbol: A crown suspended above a mirrored flame
Synopsis: The Emperor is not a god, but the anchor of divine function. Upon coronation, their will reshapes the metaphysical structure of the Solara Imperium. Domains shift, an Emperor of conquest brings War and Dominion, one of reform ushers in Knowledge and Law. Clerics bound to the Living Throne feel this change as revelation; their rites, miracles, and even their purpose realign overnight. The Empire does not worship the Emperor's person, but their current truth. When the Emperor dies, their influence is erased by the Unchaining. Only the present ruler defines the sacred rites of the Imperium.Note Divine Font: ? Divine Sanctification: ? Divine Skill: ? Favored Weapon: ? Domains: ? Cleric Spells: ?
misc/gods/gods-of-the-solara-imperium/th.sourcePath: Misc/Gods/Gods of the Solara Imperium/The Living Throne.md

misc/gods/gods-of-the-solara-imperium/th.title: The Pulse Unending
misc/gods/gods-of-the-solara-imperium/th.description: Domains: Labor, Industry, Motion, Continuity Symbol: A heart wrapped in machinery, pistons rising where veins should be Synopsis: The Pulse is the living rhythm of the Solara Imperium’s industry. It drives the machines, the schedules, the cities themselves. Every factory ticks to its beat. Every worker moves in its tempo. To resist the Pulse is to slow the machine; to slow the machine is to threaten the future. Progress is breathless, tireless, endless, and so must those in it be. Those who are attuned to the technology of the Imperium are considered blessed, for they can hear the heart thump with the Solaran Emperors divine command.Note Divine Font: ? Divine Sanctification: ? Divine Skill: ? Favored Weapon: ? Domains: ? Cleric Spells: ?
misc/gods/gods-of-the-solara-imperium/th.sourcePath: Misc/Gods/Gods of the Solara Imperium/The Pulse Unending.md

misc/gods/gods-of-the-solara-imperium/th.title: The Quiet Forge
misc/gods/gods-of-the-solara-imperium/th.description: Domains: Innovation, Discipline, Knowledge, Precision Symbol: A still anvil split by a beam of cold light Synopsis: The Quiet Forge is not a place but a principle: that discovery without restraint is ruin. In the Solara Imperium, knowledge is tempered, not pursued. Scientists work in silence, shaping advancement with careful hands. They do not seek recognition. They deliver miracles because the Imperium requires them, and because the Solaran Emperor spoke them into being.Note Divine Font: ? Divine Sanctification: ? Divine Skill: ? Favored Weapon: ? Domains: ? Cleric Spells: ?
misc/gods/gods-of-the-solara-imperium/th.sourcePath: Misc/Gods/Gods of the Solara Imperium/The Quiet Forge.md

misc/gods/gods-of-the-solara-imperium/th.title: The Silent Flow
misc/gods/gods-of-the-solara-imperium/th.description: Domains: Nature, Cycles, Survival, Subordination Symbol: A spiral of flowing lines, like water or wind Synopsis: The Silent Flow is the reverence of countless lesser spirits, rivers, storms, roots, and stones, each with its own quiet will. They are not gods, but presences, honored in gestures and rituals passed down in silence. Within the Solara Imperium, they endured, but not defied, nature made obedient. But beneath that obedience, the Flow remembers a time when it was not silent.Note Divine Font: ? Divine Sanctification: ? Divine Skill: ? Favored Weapon: ? Domains: ? Cleric Spells: ?
misc/gods/gods-of-the-solara-imperium/th.sourcePath: Misc/Gods/Gods of the Solara Imperium/The Silent Flow.md

misc/gods/gods-of-the-solara-imperium/th.title: The Steel Spire
misc/gods/gods-of-the-solara-imperium/th.description: Domains: Sacrifice, Empire, Discipline, Immortality Symbol: An upward-reaching tower formed from entwined blades and bones Synopsis: The closest thing the Solara Imperium has to a god, the Steel Spire is not a being, but a structure of spiritual resonance, its base forged from the blades of fallen heroes, its peak unseen, always reaching. Every citizen contributes to its metaphorical height by fulfilling their role in the Imperium. The Spire is said to "record" the heat of martyrdom, hard work, and servitude and transform it into divine permanence. In ritual, names of the fallen are engraved into alloy tablets, sealed in vaults near the emperor that ring like bells when war draws near.Note Divine Font: ? Divine Sanctification: ? Divine Skill: ? Favored Weapon: ? Domains: ? Cleric Spells: ?
misc/gods/gods-of-the-solara-imperium/th.sourcePath: Misc/Gods/Gods of the Solara Imperium/The Steel Spire.md

misc/gods/gods-of-the-solara-imperium/th.title: The Vessel of Intent
misc/gods/gods-of-the-solara-imperium/th.description: Domains: Purpose, Caste, Identity, Constraint Symbol: A sealed crucible stamped with an unknown sigil Synopsis: Within the Solara Imperium, purpose is not chosen—it is poured. The Vessel of Intent defines every citizen’s role at birth, not as limitation, but as clarity. To question one's function is to fracture the whole. Reassignment is rare, failure is returned to the crucible. You are what you are made to be—and nothing more. In this, there is peace.Note Divine Font: ? Divine Sanctification: ? Divine Skill: ? Favored Weapon: ? Domains: ? Cleric Spells: ?
misc/gods/gods-of-the-solara-imperium/th.sourcePath: Misc/Gods/Gods of the Solara Imperium/The Vessel of Intent.md

misc/groups/empires-and-confederations/a.title: Aetherion Corporation
misc/groups/empires-and-confederations/a.description:
Quote Wealth is wisdom, trade is destiny, and power is eternal. — High Ledger-Keeper Vorric Dain The Aetherion Corporation is a vast corporate-theocratic empire, where capital is law, trade is the highest virtue, and economic mastery is the ultimate form of conquest.
Rising from the corporations that pioneered space flight on the dwarven planet of Khaldorr Prime, it used its monopoly on space flight in order to dictate the expansion of civilization itself, not through war but by owning the very means of reaching the stars. In order to keep the planets under them from breaking contract, the Corporation decentralizes at least one necessary resource for survival from any settlement. The only ships allowed to deliver that resource to the settlement are officially sanctioned Aetherion Corporation ships.Members❄️
Abrunda Aetherforge

misc/groups/empires-and-confederations/c.title: Concord of Aedryn
misc/groups/empires-and-confederations/c.description:
Quote Some call it stagnation. I call it stability. Progress is nothing without a foundation to stand on, and we have built the most prosperous foundation in the known galaxy. President Arlen Vaest The largest of the empires, it is a federal republic where prosperity and unity mask the slow creep of bureaucracy, corporate influence, and political inertia.
Founded on the prosperous but undiscovered world of Aedryn, the Concord of Aedryn was formed through Aedryn Accords, a unification of war-torn planetary states seeking stability through trade and mutual defense. Rapid expansion followed, integrating worlds through diplomacy and economic incentives, though corporate interests soon entrenched themselves in governance. While it remains one of the wealthiest, largest, and most advanced civilizations, political stagnation and corporate overreach have begun to erode its ideals.Major Gods:
Aeryn-Vox
Drav'kyn
Eliathe
Ithex
Kael’Zar
Kelyx
Myrrhex
N’Zhereth
Quorrax
Solenne
Tarellon

Xalaith
Zarn Members❄️

Sellis Vae
Eleanor Forte

misc/groups/empires-and-confederations/h.title: Hekath
misc/groups/empires-and-confederations/h.description: Quote They are not a swarm. A swarm is mindless. They are not a plague. A plague does not choose. They are hunger made flesh, and they have already decided that we belong to them. - Grand Admiral Kethis Valorr Deadly Beasts dedicated to consuming anything that they come into contact with.The Hekath are a force of biological weapons keen on assimilation, seeking organic life only to consume and integrate it into themselves. They do not negotiate, take prisoners, or leave survivors, any being possessing DNA is killed on sight. Despite their mindless hunger, some androids have demonstrated the ability to communicate with them, though no clear motivation or purpose has ever been understood. Hekath remains reveal a truth of their nature, they do not simply devour, they evolve, incorporating the genetic traits of their prey into their own flesh. Their origins are unknown, the only current security is they do not invade in greater numbers.

misc/groups/empires-and-confederations/h.title: Hollow Stars
misc/groups/empires-and-confederations/h.description:
Quote You keep flying, you keep breathing. That's all that matters. – Ranger Saria Vale
A loose confederation of planets and solar systems at the edge of the Solara Imperium, the Concord of Aedryn, and the Aetherion Corporation.Founded during the grand expansion of those empires as a collection of free worlds attempting to retain self-autonomy, the hollow stars confederation technically has no central authority, with each planet/ecosystem providing representatives that allow negotiations between each internal government. There are some hard-set rules that all planets in the Hollow Stars need to agree with in order to be a member of the confederation. The planet must not block trade with any other planet within the confederation, the planet must allow Rangers from other planets in the confederation to hunt down criminals that fled the original planet, if one planet is attacked by an outside government body all planets must provide aid in whatever way they can to stop the whole confederacy from falling, the internal government must not recognize itself as part of a larger non-Hallow Star government

misc/groups/empires-and-confederations/r.title: Reimap
misc/groups/empires-and-confederations/r.description:
Quote
You perceive time as a river. We knew it as an ocean. Now, we float in your stream, stranded in a current we did not choose. – Received transmission from a molten planet
A formerly shapeless dimensionally reduced people, found in the folds of space they are kind but impossible to understand without training. When a foreign object entered their dimension, it introduced chaos forcing them to flee as the reality crumpled resulting in them fleeing to the corners of our system. They reside within odd places in space which are inhospitable to natives of the plane but are composed of relative pure mathematics with minimal stochastic effects to account for.

misc/groups/empires-and-confederations/s.title: Scarlet Dominion
misc/groups/empires-and-confederations/s.description:
Quote What is freedom but the luxury of decay? Kneel, and I will grant you eternity. - Archfiend Veltharion A hellish empire from the far reaches of space, bringing devils into the world and allowing them to take souls back to the devils’ home dimension.
Their queen feared entropy and the blotting of her sun, in desperate bid she contacted the hellish planes. The solar system was thus transformed into an infernal manifestation on the material plane halting much of the stochastic processes from the blotting of the sun to biological evolution, ensuring all things followed the hellish laws. Though small the empire has managed to claim 3 entire solar systems for itself and send out operatives to tempt souls to bolster their ranks and expand the empire.Members❄️
Veltharion

misc/groups/empires-and-confederations/s.title: Solara Imperium
misc/groups/empires-and-confederations/s.description:
Quote The stars belong to those who live, struggle, and die. Not to timeless, unchanging despots who fear death and deny progress. - Emperor Khael The emperor of man stands for the mortals, as a manifest dominion decree they state the stars belong to man not the unfeeling immortals.
The imperium was founded following the chaos of the Immortal Wars on the human home world. For centuries, the human home world and its colonies were ruled by cold, deathless tyrants, their decrees enforced by soulless constructs and cloned enforcers, who themselves never knew death. Thus the solar throne was formed as a war machine breaking the hold immortals had over them, it now views itself as the true inheritor of the cosmos for it was mankind alone who shattered the chains of eternity and reclaimed their right to rule. The Imperium marches forward into the stars, for to halt is to become like the stagnant immortals that claimed humanity before."Gods:"
The Ashen Veil
The Emperor Ascendant
The Hollow Chorus
The Living Throne
The Pulse Unending
The Quiet Forge
The Silent Flow
The Steel Spire
The Vessel of Intent
hate Aetherion CorporationMembers❄️
Imperior Empress (EP 0–present)
Khael (until EP 0)
Kasane Teto

misc/groups/empires-and-confederations/v.title: Voquari Ascendancy
misc/groups/empires-and-confederations/v.description:
Quote From dust, we carve divinity. From chaos, we sculpt the eternal. The Ascendancy does not conquer—it reclaims what was lost. - High Celestarch Vaelos A gilded empire of eternal rulers, obsessed with celestial ascension.The ascendency finds candidates on worlds who are ready to become celestial Solars, those Solars are then given control of the planet and allowed to transform it along with with their own celestial edicts, sculpting it into a fragment of the heavens that were lost when mortals first reached beyond the sky. In this way, the Ascendancy does not merely rule—it seeks to rebuild paradise itself, one world at a time.Members❄️
Vaelos

misc/awakening.html.title: Awakening
misc/awakening.html.description: a term given for magic powers becoming innate in someone with no/little magic powers. It appears to be caused by stress and the power appears as an alleviator of said stress. Not observed in objects as shown by the Object Stressor Experiments that were leaked in the Concord of Aedryn.

misc/cultivation.html.title: Cultivation
misc/cultivation.html.description: refining ones body, mind, and spiritual energy to ascend to a higher realm or gain power. Often the result of intense meditation, energy circulation, and visualization enhanced by Stressors, philosophical realizations, and spiritual enlightenment. It is an old tradition that faded from public consciousness in favor of technology and magic, though recently anyone can become a cultivator by buying the Ascendant package from Aetherion Corporation available in Core, Nascent Ascendant, and Immortal tiers.

misc/doctorinecasting.html.title: Doctorinecasting
misc/doctorinecasting.html.description: A form of magic where the user of immense conviction is able to bend reality so long as they truly believe it. The use of which can forge or dissolve metaphysical laws for brief moments in their vicinity. Belief must be total, doubt is fatal. Typically accompanied by confirmers, a group of people with belief or altered memory that affirm the belief allowing small leniency on the part of the caster. Very powerful but high risk, can cause Schismatic Implosions resulting in the user imploding both physically and spiritually or in Ideophantasms when they die where the belief continues to walk long after the user has died.the Solaran Emperor is believed to be one due to one advisor who was found dead claimed they were able to manifest the will of his people seemingly into production towards their goal. Though this is debated as it was a piece of information traded for asylum.Most Major factions use them in order to achieve the impossible for smaller factions and some cult leaders are said to have used it. Though sadly failing to hold the doctrine erases memory of you so there are more that you should know but dont due to that.Technically some believe the first Contemplative was one but again they're hard to verify as skeptics can usually claim it was something else unless they erased themselves in which case they aren't remembered

misc/echoforging.html.title: Echoforging
misc/echoforging.html.description: A magic that is done by doing a task meticulously the same for a stretch of time such that one day, the man becomes unnecessary and the action happens on its own. Hammers forge and vials fill themselves. Was the primary mode of factories before the invention of robotics.

misc/liturgical-communion.html.title: Liturgical Communion
misc/liturgical-communion.html.description: The process developed by monks of Aeryn-Vox during a siege where food ran thin, of eating a book page by page after reading it, a manner of ritual is preformed during the reading (secret to those outside of practicing orders) that causes the book to bind with the person allowing words to form upon their skin said to be divine revelation to those who can interpret it. In combative orders, the monks then take the empty covers of the books and convert them into magazines so that the connection between the monk and book may fuel the bullets with their magics. People with tattoos depicting words are asked to cover them up in the presence of peaceful orders and attacked on sight by the non peaceful orders.

misc/technomancy.html.title: Technomancy
misc/technomancy.html.description: the ability to sense and manipulate technology. Individuals with it can typically sense the presence of signals and with practice interpret them without the use of assistant technology. The standard spell focus for one is a signal interpreter that converts the tech manipulation of a technomancer into raw physical magic. Technomancers typically experience extreme psychological hardship when removed from a system with signal noise too long much like a nontechnomancer put into a sensory deprivation chamber and thus many carry small signal noise generators in order to combat this when going to systems with no internet.

misc/threadbinding.html.title: Threadbinding
misc/threadbinding.html.description: The use of a shared craft allow two people to induce a sympathetic bond, sensing emotions from a short distance even in the absence of technology or magic.

misc/tricaelus-conflict.html.title: Tricaelus Conflict
misc/tricaelus-conflict.html.description: involved The Concord of Aedryn, Solara Imperium, and The Aetherion Corporation, which was the last standardized decade ago fought over the Vorrakis System, Nyx-Cadion System, Zelthar System, Orphos-9 System, Tiraxis System, and Ephraem's Reach. The war was brutal fracturing the borders of each empire resulting in a stalemate, great support for abandoned solar systems to the Hollow Star Collective, and the expansion agreement. Where the empires can only expand at a shared rate with the other two, failure to expand at a similar rate will be taken as a declaration of war by the two non expanders.

misc/vacuum-rites.html.title: Vacuum Rites
misc/vacuum-rites.html.description: A series of rites done outside of the safety of a spaceship in the vacuum can take many forms. The most common of which is to do so on the darkside of a planet absent a moon in order to be able to manifest void and remove properties from objects and creatures. The most common of which tend to be the Rite of Lightless Lungs which allow one to survive for 3 days between breaths. Others that allow users to remove things such as gravity, physical interaction, and the need to speak to cast.

npcs/epitaph-protocol-npcs/asper-npcs/ko.title: Ko
npcs/epitaph-protocol-npcs/asper-npcs/ko.description:
Siryu's cousin
They were bubbly and easy to get along with, but didn’t seem fit for constant space travel and decided to settle down in the next big port that they stopped at. At one point, ASPER offered to bring them into their bond, but they shut that down without hesitation.

npcs/epitaph-protocol-npcs/asper-npcs/na.title: Naeri Astana
npcs/epitaph-protocol-npcs/asper-npcs/na.description:
Family❄️Adoptive mother: Shūjiǎo (EP -158–EP -21)
Adopted child: ASPER (b. EP -18)Timeline❄️📋EP -41age 0 — Born-25age 2067 — Adopted
EP -18age 23 — Adopted ASPER
(She/her, 41y/o, alive) - A lashunta diplomat with connections to the Concord of Aedryn and follower of Drav'kyn. She was close with Shujiao before her cultivation accident, and took her in when she expressed her wish to leave. During the renewal period, Naeri grew increasingly worried about the state of Shujiao's body and attempted to get assistance in repairing it, but very little could be done beyond making sure that the deterioration didn't worsen. When ASPER took over the body, she was relieved and did her best to fill them in on any questions that they might have. To her surprise, they took quick interest in Aeryn-Vox without any prompting from her, so she very tentatively took them back to the Song Tian Temple that Shujiao lived for the majority of her life.
After about a yearish there, ASPER got her to take them back with her on her ship after a disagreement between them and Velin Desat.
She introduced them to Siryu. Since then, the two of them have been working odd jobs here and there transporting goods under the radar for her.

npcs/epitaph-protocol-npcs/asper-npcs/ra.title: Raz
npcs/epitaph-protocol-npcs/asper-npcs/ra.description: The first loss of someone in their bond. ASPER was young and had made tentative ‘friends’ with a cultivator that kept in contact after they left the temple. Her name was Raz, a human who had grown up in the Temple. She was very quiet and kept to herself for the most part, but the two kept in communication despite it. One moment she was there, a constant in the bond, and the next, gone. ASPER quickly contacted Velin Desat, who revealed that Raz got caught up in a fire fight while out purchasing supplies and no one was there to help.

npcs/epitaph-protocol-npcs/asper-npcs/sh.title: Shūjiǎo
npcs/epitaph-protocol-npcs/asper-npcs/sh.description:
Family❄️Adopted child: Naeri Astana (b. EP -41)Timeline❄️📋EP -158age 0 — Born
-25age 2184 — Adopted Naeri AstanaEP -21age 137 — Died in Aedryn, Aedryn System(she/her, ~137 when passed the body along) -
The old owner of ASPER's body, and a cleric of Aeryn-Vox. She was part of a group of cultivators (Sòng Tiān Temple) who were trying to bring about a new age of 'immortality' by integrating artificial beings into their practices. At some point before releasing her body, her cultivation caused a full body malfunction, changing her from a very kind and outspoken person into someone that became more reserved and ended up writing her thoughts down into several journals rather than speaking them aloud. She left the temple with Naeri Astana not long after, and then passed these journals along to her to give to the next owner of her body before she underwent renewal. During renewal, there was a 3 year period of time where no new soul entered, and the body began to break down further.
knew abt Data-Temples
body has gone through Renewal

npcs/epitaph-protocol-npcs/asper-npcs/si.title: Siryu
npcs/epitaph-protocol-npcs/asper-npcs/si.description:
(she/her, 22 y/o, alive) - A pahtra ship pilot. She's mostly known for running a beat up piece of garbage that she lovingly calls the Rusty Curiosity, but when she's working, it runs like a beauty. She specializes in working under the radar, which leads her to transporting things most people would raise an eyebrow to. She also takes on passengers for the right price. However, with the nature of her jobs, she needed someone to help her out, which led to Naeri Astana recommending ASPER to take up the job. What they didn't realize was that the job was basically just a smuggling gig, but they quickly adjusted once the confusion was cleared up.
hired by Xep's Boss to pick up E-Xep-Ro, got blamed for the death of Arroyo Cemsor at The Mirralight and was detained in Thorne
knew Shujiao?

npcs/epitaph-protocol-npcs/asper-npcs/ve.title: Velin Desat
npcs/epitaph-protocol-npcs/asper-npcs/ve.description:
(he/him, 340y/o, alive) - A blind vlaka who was raised within the temple and who has spent the past 20 years as sect leader of Song Tian Temple. He very briefly knew Shujiao before she left, so when ASPER returned, they were welcomed with open arms. However, ASPER quickly left barely a year later after a disagreement between them broke out over the purpose of cultivation; he believed that it is only when you ascend to immortality that you realize your true potential in the world, while ASPER felt that ascension was not necessary to achieve that. He has tried bringing them back after their argument, and even sent some cultivators after them, but ASPER did not want to go back. Despite this, they still keep him in their mystic bond. Relatively tense relationship between them.
Cultivation
Aeryn-Vox
aliases: Velin Desat of circle Song Tian Temple

pcs-(active)/asper.html.title: ASPER
pcs-(active)/asper.html.description:
Family❄️Adoptive mother: Naeri Astana (b. EP -41)
Shūjiǎo (EP -158–EP -21)Timeline❄️📋EP -18age 0 — Born in Aedryn, Aedryn SystemEP -18age 0 — Adopted
EP 0age 18 — Start of Epitaph Protocol in Kiliv-VI
Pinterest
Sheet (lvls 1-2)
Age: 18 Body age: 158 years
body has gone through RenewalSPOILERS Hatsune miku the starlight idol almado ripley nivaya e-xep-ro the 26 laws of power TLDR: ASPER (they/them) Android mystic actively breaking down due to body malfunctions that have damaged their emotion processors
Body used to be owned by Shujiao (she/her), a cultivator + cleric of Aeryn-Vox Based on Shujiao’s writings, they have been trying to find a data-temple that might contain info on how to prevent continued deterioration Has been acting as a smuggler to slowly fund their way between worlds Who are you now? ASPER (they/them) Android mystic who is actively deteriorating Why are you the class you are? What drew you to this path instead of another? Instead of being drawn to Aeryn-Vox as a cleric, they felt more drawn to what she represents, rather than her as a divine being overall. What do you specialize in? Combat, science, mysticism, tech, diplomacy, etc.? mysticism, religion What are your personal edicts and anathemas? Describe a situation that might challenge or complicate those beliefs. Edicts: Patience and persistence in all things. Pursue the truth no matter what. Relentlessly document and interpret data. Anathemas: Fail to safeguard magical texts, artifacts or knowledge. Engage in overly unhealthy or self-destructive behaviors. Hold a grudge. If the information that needs to be documented could dramatically disrupt the lives of the people in their mystic bond. If it's choosing between those close to them over magical texts. If patience would backfire tremendously. What are your current goals? What do you want to change or preserve in the galaxy? They want to share the information locked away in the data temples; they believe that if she didn't want the temples to be sought after that she wouldn't have marked them with her symbols and left them as a sort of puzzle to find.
What circumstances led you to traveling through the fringes of space near the nominally Hollow Star planet of Kiliv-VI?
Naeri actually contacted ASPER and suggested it based on information she received; she strongly implied that it would lead them in the right direction of a data temple without outright saying it. They “borrowed” Siryu’s ship in the meantime. How did you gain your class abilities? Formal training, private sector enhancement, military service, experimentation, accident, natural talent? Technically natural talent? It was more of a natural extension of learning about Shujiao; she became wholly devoted to Aeryn-Vox, and while they feel strongly connected to their shared god, they feel more attuned to what she represents rather than the goddess herself. Who were you before your current role or journey? A failed diplomat. They shadowed Naeri for a time being, but that was quickly shut down once their seemingly uncaring interactions caused tensions to rise and a few bullets shot. They were asked not to do that again. Are you aligned with any organizations? What did you do before joining up with that organization? What is your strongest memory from earlier in your life? Are there memories you’ve lost or that haunt you?
The first loss of someone in their bond. ASPER was young and had made tentative ‘friends’ with a cultivator that kept in contact after they left the temple. Her name was Raz, a human who had grown up in the temple. She was very quiet and kept to herself for the most part, but the two kept in communication despite it. One moment she was there, a constant in the bond, and the next, gone. ASPER quickly contacted Velin, who revealed that Raz got caught up in a fire fight while out purchasing supplies and no one was there to help. If your life had gone differently, what would you be doing now?
Entirely possibly they’d be back at the temple working with Velin. Potentially even becoming sect leader should they have properly pursued cultivation instead of learning the beliefs behind it and leaving with no proper practice. Where were you born or raised? Planet, station, habitat, ship, etc.?
Technically on Naeri’s ship, but also partially on the small planet of [insert name here] at the Sòng Tiān Temple. Most of their life has been spent on ships, however. Who were you closest to in your past?
For a while there was another person helping out Siryu: Siryu’s cousin, Ko. They were bubbly and easy to get along with, but didn’t seem fit for constant space travel and decided to settle down in the next big port that they stopped at. At one point, ASPER offered to bring them into their bond, but they shut that down without hesitation. Who did you consider your family—biological, adopted, and/or chosen? Naeri basically raised ASPER as a mother, teaching them how to be someone while also grieving Shujiao. Describe a moment or event that deeply shaped who you are now. After leaving Song Tian Temple, ASPER fell into a bit of a rut for a few years and struggled to figure out what to be doing. During this time, Naeri grew increasingly concerned and eventually brough out one of Shujiao’s journals and passed it along to them. They read through it and was completely fascinated with what they found there. Naeri hesitated for a while to give another, but when they saw how ASPER found a purpose with tracking what Shujiao did in life, she kept giving them book after book. There are still several that she hasn’t passed along from the end of Shujiao’s life that ASPER has been vying for for years. Name three NPCs from your past that you still think about. Are they alive? Where are they now?
(Bonus since important. lol) Shūjiǎo (书角) (she/her, ~137 when passed the body along) - The old owner of ASPER's body, and a cleric of Aeryn-Vox. She was part of a group of cultivators (Sòng Tiān (送天) Temple) who were trying to bring about a new age of 'immortality' by integrating artificial beings into their practices. At some point before releasing her body, her cultivation caused a full body malfunction, changing her from a very kind and outspoken person into someone that became more reserved and ended up writing her thoughts down into several journals rather than speaking them aloud. She left the temple with Naeri Astana not long after, and then passed these journals along to her to give to the next owner of her body before she underwent renewal. During renewal, there was a 3 year period of time where no new soul entered, and the body began to break down further.
Naeri Astana (She/her, 41y/o, alive) - A lashunta diplomat with connections to the Concord of Aedryn and follower of Drav'kyn. She was close with Shujiao before her cultivation accident, and took her in when she expressed her wish to leave. During the renewal period, Naeri grew increasingly worried about the state of Shujiao's body and attempted to get assistance in repairing it, but very little could be done beyond making sure that the deterioration didn't worsen. When ASPER took over the body, she was relieved and did her best to fill them in on any questions that they might have. To her surprise, they took quick interest in Aeryn-Vox without any prompting from her, so she very tentatively took them back to the temple that Shujiao lived for the majority of her life. After about a yearish there, ASPER got her to take them back with her on her ship after a disagreement between them and Velin. She introduced them to Siryu. Since then, the two of them have been working odd jobs here and there transporting goods under the radar for her.
Velin Desat (Velin Desat of circle Song Tian Temple) (he/him, 34y/o, alive) - A blind vlaka who was raised within the temple and who has spent the past 20 years as sect leader. He very briefly knew Shujiao before she left, so when ASPER returned, they were welcomed with open arms. However, ASPER quickly left barely a year later after a disagreement between them broke out over the purpose of cultivation; he believed that it is only when you ascend to immortality that you realize your true potential in the world, while ASPER felt that ascension was not necessary to achieve that. He has tried bringing them back after their argument, and even sent some cultivators after them, but ASPER did not want to go back. Despite this, they still keep him in their mystic bond. Relatively tense relationship between them.
Siryu (she/her, 22 y/o, alive) - A pahtra ship pilot. She's mostly known for running a beat up piece of garbage that she lovingly calls the Rusty Curiosity, but when she's working, it runs like a beauty. She specializes in working under the radar, which leads her to transporting things most people would raise an eyebrow to. She also takes on passengers for the right price. However, with the nature of her jobs, she needed someone to help her out, which led to Naeri recommending ASPER to take up the job. What they didn't realize was that the job was basically just a smuggling gig, but they quickly adjusted once the confusion was cleared up. Do you have any prior connection to any of the other PCs?
The 26 Laws of Power
Ended up being on the Rusty Curiosity for a time where ASPER sat down near them and kept asking questions. They’d get a response, they’d type it down, pause, and then ask another question. They didn't get to interact with many new people, so finding new information was welcomed regardless of who it came from. Mystic Bond - Up to 10 willing creatures; i always know general direction and HP totals, if concious/unconscious, dying, or dead. At 3rd, this also extends to other members of the bond with each other. Naeri Astana Velin Desat Siryu Party Member #1 Party Member #2 Party Member #3 Party Member #4 Party Member #5 Party Member #6 What themes or story arcs are you hoping to explore with this character? What it means to be religious despite the fact that they are an artificial being Dealing with those who recognize 'you' when their idea of who you are and who you are being two different things What kind of gameplay experience do you want mechanically? (e.g. utility, heavy hitter, problem solver, weird build) healer + utility As this is the flagship campaign in the new world how would you prefer technical terms be given out? maybe a basic "heres some things your characters would know" while the rest comes up naturally through questions during game and such. if i am understanding this correctly lmfao What are you hoping to see in this campaign? (Tone, themes, types of encounters or problems) Truth vs lies? Haunting the narrative? I’m kind of struggling to come up with an answer here What content or themes would you prefer not to be included? ideally avoiding explicit depiction of animal death would be good and hurting children This campaign will include themes of extortion/slavery, control, death, and post/transhumanism. Are there any specific types of loss or emotional beats (e.g. betrayal, body horror, loss of autonomy) that are off-limits for you? a heads up for specifically eye damage would be good, but otherwise i am good! There are some horrors innately related to space (e.g. loneliness, time distortion, etc) is there any special way you'd prefer I broach these topics? no! i'm good

misc/gods/gods-of-the-aetherion-corporat.title: Aulgrith Narvaghel
misc/gods/gods-of-the-aetherion-corporat.description: God of Monsters, Mutation, and Evolution Without Mercy. Worshipped by biotech heretics and radical adaptives. “What survives is holy.”

misc/gods/gods-of-the-aetherion-corporat.title: Dravok Thuldros
misc/gods/gods-of-the-aetherion-corporat.description: God of Warriors and Strength. Patron of iron battalions that take to the sky protecting the vulnerable.
misc/gods/gods-of-the-aetherion-corporat.sourcePath: Misc/Gods/Gods of the Aetherion Corporation/Dravok Thuldros.md

misc/gods/gods-of-the-aetherion-corporat.title: Dulgrim Varnhollow
misc/gods/gods-of-the-aetherion-corporat.description: God of Death, Pyres, and Legacy. Keeps records of every fallen soul and the debts they carried with them to the grave. One must be sure to carry enough credits to pay their way to the desired afterlife.
misc/gods/gods-of-the-aetherion-corporat.sourcePath: Misc/Gods/Gods of the Aetherion Corporation/Dulgrim Varnhollow.md

misc/gods/gods-of-the-aetherion-corporat.title: Ezrith Vaerwyn
misc/gods/gods-of-the-aetherion-corporat.description: Goddess of Endings, Collapsing Stars, and the Yndra. One day it is said Khaldûr-Zin will be dust spread to the galactic winds.
misc/gods/gods-of-the-aetherion-corporat.sourcePath: Misc/Gods/Gods of the Aetherion Corporation/Ezrith Vaerwyn.md

misc/gods/gods-of-the-aetherion-corporat.title: Ghuldran
misc/gods/gods-of-the-aetherion-corporat.description: God of the Forge and Creation, creator of the dwarfs according to ancient tradition he came to the planet with a hammer and forged them from perfect stone and metal. The origin of worth its weight in Gold in the common tongue

misc/gods/gods-of-the-aetherion-corporat.description: Goddess of Home and Defense, her symbols etched above airlock doors and emergency bunkers to ward off breach and despair.

places/ship/the-serenade/the-serenade.ht.title: The Serenade
places/ship/the-serenade/the-serenade.ht.description:
The loss of The Serenade was a casino in deep space meant to be outside of the view of official governments. Some said it was a deep security prison for something that needed to be viewed at all times but before it could be confirmed, The Serenade disappeared without a trace. Sexico-6
Slotsnax

places/khaldûr-zin.html.title: Khaldûr-Zin
places/khaldûr-zin.html.description: There is only two entrances and exits to Khaldûr-Zin, once those were claimed by Aetherion Corporation competition ceased to exist and all of the planet was diverted to a new goal, growth of this.due to the spinning rings above the planet that keep the surface at the roche limit there are only two ways in or out of the atmosphere, thus if you want access to outerspace you need to be an Aetherion Vessel once that was decided it only took 100 years to strangle the competition inside

sessions/oneshots/serenade-heist/serenad.title: Serenade Heist
sessions/oneshots/serenade-heist/serenad.description:
PCS: A Envoy
Phoenix
Sarah Technomancer
Threllok-Zirshus
Vanx
Wanwan Sessions:
Session 1
Sessions/Oneshots/Serenade Heist/Session 2
Sessions/Oneshots/Serenade Heist/Session 3

sessions/oneshots/serenade-heist/session.title: Session 1
sessions/oneshots/serenade-heist/session.description:
Next: Session 2
A Envoy
Phoenix
Sarah Technomancer
Threllok-Zirshus
Vanx
Wanwan
The party has been hired to infiltrate The Serenade by a contact called Greying Twilight. He is an older gentleman who will be waiting for you when you arrive in the Slotsnax backroom. The goal is simple, held in the turret by the Big Pits quarters is a data drive that you only need to hit release on. The problem is if you want to do this and make it out alive you're going to need to disable security from getting to you, have a sample of the Big Pits' DNA, and set a bomb on the generators that will disable most automatic systems not on the backup grid.
Honny J is a thief with a lead on Big Pit’s DNA Main floor with his robot companion
Sentient slot machine Eac-A Kind of a roguish fellow, looks like he hasn’t showered in days Wears a letterman jacket with the letter Q We need to destroy at least 2 of 4 generators Can bribe chief of staff
He likes to spend time away from his guards at Sexico-6 Can destroy all the police vehicles Shunt them out the airlock Plainclothes operatives are all around the casino We go down to the docks and we hijack a ship to go check out the heat vents Seems like an air lock - they build up noxious gas and then launch it into the gas giant below Collection device below the rungs, collecting fumes from the gas giant and sucking them in We could go in but if we time it wrong it would vaporize us A skiff getting jammed in the air lock would stop the generator from working We’re going to be able to get into one generator through either the chief engineer or the chief of security First we find Honny J and Eac-a We find him out on the floor pretty easily We’re gonna go talk with the big rig cannon It fires into the gas giant whenever anyone gets a jackpot We really don’t have anything to trade with this guy for the info He uses the info as his ticket to gamble here forever We could theoretically act like we already have the dna and bluff hard enough that big pit comes to confront us when we need the DNA If he thinks we have it he’ll confront us, if he doesn’t believe it he won’t We manage to get a tour of one of the generator wings 1 person could run it but there are 15 people stationed The lightning tubes are super delicate, a single person could break them
Few odd mannerisms from Rank, tries to blend in with his workers, incredibly quick on the draw from his finger pistols, grew in village of humans (opposite of Karn), runs the auxiliary wings, Big Pit runs his own personal wing inside, personality “jives well with the men” (they hate his ass), leads to control rooms, panels, v clear he lacks knowledge about the technical stuff
Phlox tells detailed stories about “Mandalrhjeim” (AAAAAAAAAA- Kieran) He actually loves a wizard WanWan is trying to leave Going through databases rn, looking at security files and debugging settings Can let you disable security valves on actual generator, not actually renew, override next time they do this to take multiple hours Download maps of the area, can find routes getting past 2 of the bars stopping stealth drone (out of 5) Eac-a……………………

sessions/oneshots/serenade-heist/session.title: Session 2
sessions/oneshots/serenade-heist/session.description:
Prev: Session 1
Next: Session 3
The Serenade
RC drone with warning to Big Pits of “hey we took your dna” Takes about 2 hours for the first signs of a reaction North wing, contains the container yard we got a skiff from Security disappears among the container yards and can’t be directly followed for about 1.5 hours A few bits of frost sticking to their armor on return Probably takes a lot of power to keep things that cold? Where is that energy going in the container yard? Threllok We leave 1k credits for breaking an nda <3 Deep storage section 4, 5; container white Storage container has the sounds of a generator coming from 10 feet below Extremely short conex with stairs that go downwards and a computer that immediately asks for the password 1 minute timer A radiation drake beats us up and makes Threllok nauseated for the foreseeable future We find a lot of blood vials labeled as “BP 1-60” Looks like this is for the spell Clone We’re approached by an uplifted bear who has video of us in the cargo hold Bounty hunter who wants us to go loud so they can kill us and get a higher bounty If we do it sneakily enough they might not know We talk to our contractor We’re currently on big pit 45 No guarantee that is 1:1 with BP45 vial? We could challenge the bounty hunter in front of the turrets
We go to Sexico-6 and have a groupon to get in We pay 1000 credits to see the giantesses of sexico 6 We negotiate with the giantess who doesn’t want to give up her most loyal client We lie that we just want to keep her and her loyal guy out of trouble “We aren’t the ones causing the problems, we’re just aware and want to make a profit” She brings out a dwarven man in a bathrobe

sessions/oneshots/serenade-heist/session.title: Session 3
sessions/oneshots/serenade-heist/session.description:
Prev: Session 2 Jamming a skiff in one generator, overriding another? Using the chief of security to make sure no guards go to our destination We smear some dna on the thingy and then we head in
The Serenade Discussing with the dwarven security officer Blackmail him with a deepfaked video of him sucking toes We’re knocking him out, doing surgery to take control of his microchip (to direct guards away,) and stick him in a cargo container to avoid him being found Our driving is incredible <3 we bypass a whole Situation <3 We got that chip and leave him in a cargo container We have around 3 days before it gets suspicious but thankfully we do not need 3 days <3 We go to the server room. Is full of ghost. Wanwan plays multiplanar subway surfers on the monitors and hacks in a little corner 5 min delay Vanx is set up to fuck shit up! Yaay Flying skiffs into systems <3 There is a critical error In 5 minutes the power will go out and our way out of this area is an elevator We did it 🎉 We have sabotaged the generators We send out the orders to the guards but don’t have the receiver so can’t tell their reactions The orders were successful, no guards just turrets without enough energy to shoot We sneak into the elevator and use the 45th’s blood It tells us “welcome back handsome” 10 minute elevator ride Clearly created to admire the rest of the casino
Elevator intercom comes on and Big Pits is suspicious of us We do a little lie but it’s quite bad He wants to send us down so we’re going for it
Greying Twilight is a fanatic of the Virtual God? Ancient civilization that learned how to digitize a person Created a civil war between physical and digital beings, destroyed their society Some people worship one side or the other Created a lot of cloud computing nanochips that spread around the galaxy and allowed for space ages around there We jump out of the elevator as zack throws a grenade in Big pit is in a giant mech suit and is now large

misc/media/games/league-of-leisure.html.title: League of Leisure
misc/media/games/league-of-leisure.html.description:
League of leisure is a moba competitive farming simulation game. Two teams of 5 players compete on a shared map to out-farm the other team by territory control and economic gain.
reach the economic gain threshold before the enemy team
TRIGGER AN ECONOMIC COLLAPSE IN THE ENEMY TEAM (DESTROY THEIR MARKET HUB) Cultivator: high-hp, zone denial units
Harvester: low-hp, accelerates food production
Merchant: low-hp, increases economic gain
Raider: mid-hp, roamer that disrupts enemy infrastructure and collects OR STEALS resources
Apothecary: mid-hp, healer for units & farm plots

misc/miunctios.html.title: Miunctios
misc/miunctios.html.description: A cult belief in the ascension of the mind through micro digitization, the most popular form of practice involves using credits' ability to mimic organic life to replace cells in the body turning the person inorganic overtime, though a fully completed version has yet to be documented. In most forms of Miunctios the body is left behind after the attempted process and the now digitized soul/mind would be free to wander the virtual paradise. There are internal conflicts in the religion of the holiness of androids/souled AIs, as some view them as divine messengers and others as traitors who have returned to the world of the physical after a successful ascension.

pcs-(active)/haven.html.title: Haven
pcs-(active)/haven.html.description:
Family❄️Father: Nel Samsal Teadri of Clan RunsaTimeline❄️📋EP -26age 0 — Born
EP 0age 26 — Start of Red Tally in Senaria, Auver System

Ithax
Senaria Suns, League of Leisure
Suimin, Denpakant
drinks Hero Energy DrinkSPOILERS
Who are you now, only weeks after The Scarring? Haven, he/him, kasatha envoy, 26y/o Why are you the class you are? What made you choose this path over another? He has a very demanding personality, and he specializes in making others do what he wants. What do you specialize in: combat, survival, influence, craft, mysticism, technology, or something else? Influence + combat Where is your Scar? Back of the neck up the back of the head. Peaks up just behind his mask. What are your personal edicts and anathemas? Describe a situation during or after The Scarring that did/could test or shatter them. Edicts Outshine your rivals. Use every resource you have to win. When the plan is falling apart, know when to cut your losses. Anathemas Breaking a sworn vow or contract. Resisting change or refusing to adapt to new circumstances. Allow personal feelings to cloud judgment. Before the shattering, he was consistently tested on his anger management skills, but he never did anything drastic that cost his team the mission. Yet. Then again, he’s actually got people he cares about now that have an even higher chance at getting killed. What is your immediate goal now that the initial killings are over? What do you most want to protect, change, or destroy in the city? Survival is the biggest thing; making sure that Ithax doesn’t get in over his head with his investigations. He wants to shut down a lot of the criminal orgs here, making sure that others weren’t dragged in like he was. How did you survive The Scarring: was it through your own hand, someone else taking the fall for you, a deal you struck, or something stranger? He struck a deal with an old acquaintance, Meskiv. She needed help getting an ex-client out of the picture, and he needed to live. He didn't even ask for their name, just knew they were some barathu. But they surely weren’t important enough if their bodyguard left as soon as things got tough. They just cried out when they got cornered. At least it was quick. How did you gain your class abilities: formal training, gang work, religious order, guild apprenticeship, military service, experimentation, accident, or natural talent? Initially gang work, but acting as a shot caller has been nice for upkeep on skills. Who were you before The Scarring began? A pro esports player, technically, but tried to hide previous involvement with criminal orgs. It’s not hard to tell what he used to do, though. When he did an occasional stream, it became a popular joke amongst his chat on what he used to do: hired thug, drug trafficking, assassination work, all were thrown out as suggestions.
He grew up in Denpakant with the name Vihe (v-eye-hey) Nanse Jaltra of Clan Runsa with his dad (Nel Samsal Teadri of Clan Runsa), but once he was old enough to start earning some credits for simple work, his dad needed more and more money for his growing gambling addiction. After shit hit the fan one too many times, Haven left their apartment at around 15 for a grimey, tiny one rented to him dirt cheap as long as he acted as errand boy for his landlord, Luthien. Turns out, he’d unknowingly joined a gang. Were you connected to any gangs, guilds, noble houses, religious orders, militias, or other organizations before all this?
A local off-shoot of a gang that called themselves the Storm. He always thought it was a stupid and cliche name, but they seemed proud of themselves, and he didn’t really want to get his ass beat. What did you do for them? It started off small; make deliveries, debt collect, don’t die. Then as leadership slowly left or got axed, his roles got more important (mostly killing people who pissed off the upper guys), until he had a small squad of guys who listened to what he had to say. And as their influence grew, that meant more people died under his watch, even some of the new guys that he said he’d take care of. Being stuck in there for just about a decade, he had his fill and decided he’d had enough. So, he cut his losses (not without some yelling and a few new bullet scars in his right arms), moved across town, changed his name, and didn’t really touch the scene until Ithax started poking his head into things. He’s lucky he’s got someone like Haven there to keep an eye on him. What is your strongest memory from before The Scarring? Has it changed in meaning since then? The first time something made him falter in his actions. He was barely 17, still a nobody, but trust was put in him to get Luthien’s money from a “tenant” that was refusing to pay. It was a vesk woman and her teenage son, just a few years younger than him at the time. He shook her, and the boy ran at him, yelling. Panic made him shoot, and the next thing he knew was the boy writhing in pain, clutching his leg. They didn’t have the money to get help, and they all knew it. He threw up in the alley outside their apartment. If the curse had never happened, what would you be doing right now? Streaming, or if he got bored, leaving the planet to find something new to do. There’s only so much you can do in one city for your entire life, especially when you’re trying to avoid the consequences of your actions. Where in the city were you born or raised or are you from offworld? He was born and raised in the southwestern district [INSERT DISTRICT NAME HERE]; it’s where his family lived for most of their lives, and he hadn’t moved far from home initially. But after he left, he researched and booked it to Denpakant (raised in district where it then nuclear meltdown. Hard 2 get to) Who were you closest to before The Scarring? Ithax, Meskiv Who do you still have left? From his understanding, his dad is still around, though it’s a bit surprising. He’d texted him, something he did often asking for money or trying to check in with him, but all it said was “Don’t come home.” What were some life changing memories before The Scarring? The first few days after leaving home. He still hadn’t found a place to stay yet, and he hadn’t thought things through that much at his age. He ran out of food super quick, so he turned to asking people politely, and when that failed, he stole. It didn’t take Luthien long to find a kid willing to do anything for a place to stay. Describe a moment from The Scarring’s first weeks that permanently changed you. Ever since he’d moved away from Storm, he’d found peace in the anonymity of a new home and faces. But that peace shattered when someone followed him home and tried to stab him in the stairwell. He’d seen the fear on their face when he pulled a pistol out and shot with precision. His life or theirs, and he won. Guess he still had the reflexes, despite trying to avoid that kind of life. Name three NPCs you know personally who also survived The Scarring. Are they allies, rivals, or something in between?
Meskiv Ocano - ysoki, she/her, behavioral scientist with a focus on criminology. She was, ironically, a major client of Storm’s. Two of her brothers were in the group, and when Haven came and made sure they didn’t die for being stupid, she was a bit soft on him. She kept in contact after he left, and though he’s pretty sure she has alternative reasons for doing so, he does appreciate a little bit of care being sent his way.
Luthien - human, he/they, 35y/o, asshole landlord that pressured Haven into joining up with Storm. Trained him in the loosest way one could interpret the word, but always bragged about the fact that Haven very rarely got his ass beat by those outside the group. Kind of his father figure (since he somewhat raised him) if his dad sucked even more and threatened him constantly.
Imrek Sariin - kasatha, he/him. Was also part of Storm, but joined after Haven. He looked up to Haven a lot, though didn’t really listen to him when he was told that he could have a better life elsewhere. Where one stayed, the other left, but Imrek has reached out to him and warned him about things that could involve him in the past. Of those three, who would you kill to save yourself? Who would you die before harming? Kill to save himself - Luthien, Imrek? Die before harming - Meskiv Do you have any history with the other PCs: family, rival, ally, co-worker, neighbor, debtor/creditor, or something else?
Ithax - Roommates and teammate. Haven tagged along on his investigations to provide back-up in case anything happened, and also to make sure that things went Ithax’s way.
Konbini-chan - Guest player on the team, know her personal identity? What themes or arcs are you hoping to explore with this character in the aftermath of The Scarring? (i’m so bad at deciding these sorts of things) People can surprise you even if you think you know who they are To what lengths can someone change who they are? How long does it take after fucking people over can you say that you’ve become a good person? What kind of gameplay experience do you want mechanically? (e.g. social manipulator, heavy hitter, problem solver, specialist) Problem solver, social manipulator. Lot of commanding my allies and spooking my enemies What kind of end do you imagine for your character: survival, sacrifice, power, transformation, or something else? I kind of actually picture sacrifice for him? How would you prefer the city’s political and faction details be delivered: in-game through NPCs, as handouts, or through GM recaps? Via NPCs or handouts, tho I prefer NPCs What are you hoping to see in this campaign? (Tone, themes, types of encounters or problems) Pushing the PCs to their limits of what they have to do in order to survive. What content or themes do you want avoided? Explicit pet death This campaign will explore coercion, control, scarcity, death, and power struggles. Are there specific emotional beats (e.g. betrayal, body horror, loss of autonomy, desperation) you want excluded? Just avoid explicitly talking abt damage to eyes pretty please! Some elements will include urban horror (claustrophobia, paranoia, constant surveillance). Is there a particular way you’d like these approached? Nope, I’m good! .
Storm has been moving up in the world getting to be headquartered in the Vitiae district; they've fallen under the command of one Mafilus, a vesk commander. Running the district as a military junta. Allegedly unkillable, he became known as the one permitted immortal of the imperium before disappearing for 10 (earth) years until The Scarring.
The internet has become localized SCRBLEs have been cut off and disallowed to enter the system. If you send out a message out of the system, it will take 6 years to get to the nearest star. Use of Remembrance Streams is also incredibly cut off, only essential personnel from each sector empire are allowed through and not allowed to make contact.
The twilight vigilante hero of Senaria, Captain Dusk, and his teen sidekick, Dawn, have disappeared. Leaving behind a power vacuum in the Grenthol district. Rumors have spread that the Vitiae district was once a military compound was an experiment to mix military infrastructure into civilian sectors. There is some truth to this due to the new tech the broken standard has suddenly acquired on the streets. As the skies are closely monitored it stands to reason they were already planet side.
aliases: Vihe Nanse Jaltra of Clan Runsa

places/systems/auver-system/auver-system.title: Auver System
places/systems/auver-system/auver-system.description:
Concord of Aedryn spacehas mega cities with great earth-like habitatsAuver System which is a border system with of 5 empires that connect to it currently under the Concord of Aedryn. Your planet is the 6th one from the sun and tidally locked, Senaria. The habitable zone is thus in the constant twilight belt, with the part that faces the sun reaching up to 400 Fahrenheit, while the permanent nightside has temperatures of around -200 Fahrenheit.The megacity is primarily built in the form of a giant line that circles the planet in the twilight zone. Commonly subdivided into 32 zones following the compass. Officially referred to by their magnetic designator, but commonly referred to common names by the populace.Your district is the in the northwest known as Denpakant.
Senaria
